title: Fake Review Detection Signals
description: Multi-dimensional signal definitions for fake review detection

signals.name: Time Cluster
signals.description: Multiple reviews posted within a short time window
signals.pattern: ≥5 reviews within 30 min or ≥8 within 1 hour

signals.name: Reviewer Account Age
signals.description: Accounts created very recently posting reviews
signals.score_func: max(0, 20 - age_days) if age_days < 7 else 10 if age_days < 30 else 0

signals.name: Review Frequency
signals.description: Abnormally high review posting rate across restaurants

signals.name: Text Similarity
signals.description: High textual similarity with other reviews for the same restaurant
signals.pattern: Cosine similarity >0.7 with multiple other reviews

signals.name: Photo EXIF Analysis
signals.description: Identical camera/settings across supposedly different users' photos
signals.pattern: ≥3 reviews with photos from same camera model/settings

signals.name: Generic Language
signals.description: Vague, template-like language without specific details
signals.pattern: Uses only generic phrases without dish names or specific descriptions

signals.name: Rating Pattern
signals.description: All 5-star reviews with no critical feedback
signals.pattern: Multiple 5-star reviews with <20 words and no specific detail

signals.name: Reviewer History
signals.description: Reviewer only posts positive reviews, never critical
signals.pattern: Reviewer has ≥5 reviews, all 4-5 stars, no critical feedback

high_risk.label: High risk - likely extensive fake review activity

global_flags.flag_message: Over 40% of reviews appear fake — strong warning
